Data sources & how to reproduce





All figures on this page are fetched from the New Jersey Department of Education via the open-source njschooldata R package. Nothing is hand-entered or estimated.
library(njschooldata)
fetch_enr(2026, tidy = TRUE) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "8140")   # enrollment
fetch_grad_rate(2025) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "8140")          # graduation
fetch_tges(2025)                                                       # spending (TGES)
Suppression: small cells are suppressed at the source by NJ DOE and appear as missing. Spending uses TGES “actuals”; peer comparisons use District Factor Group (DFG) peers.

Data sources & how to reproduce





All figures on this page are fetched from the New Jersey Department of Education via the open-source njschooldata R package. Nothing is hand-entered or estimated.
library(njschooldata)
fetch_enr(2026, tidy = TRUE) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "5105")   # enrollment
fetch_grad_rate(2025) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "5105")          # graduation
fetch_tges(2025)                                                       # spending (TGES)
Suppression: small cells are suppressed at the source by NJ DOE and appear as missing. Spending uses TGES “actuals”; peer comparisons use District Factor Group (DFG) peers.

library(njschooldata)
fetch_enr(2026, tidy = TRUE) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "3960")   # enrollment
fetch_grad_rate(2025) |> dplyr::filter(district_id == "3960")          # graduation
fetch_tges(2024)                                                       # spending (TGES)
Suppression: small cells are suppressed at the source by NJ DOE and appear as missing. Spending uses TGES “actuals”; peer comparisons use District Factor Group (DFG) peers.

title: New Jersey District Profiles
text:
Every number on these pages comes straight from the New Jersey Department of Education, fetched with the open-source njschooldata engine. Pick a district to see its enrollment, demographics, spending, outcomes, and school-climate data, with every metric benchmarked against its District Factor Group peers. Nothing here is estimated or hand-entered.
683 districts  ·  1,357,656 students statewide  ·  683 profiles published so far









 Back to top

title: About the Data
text: Every figure on this site is fetched directly from the New Jersey Department of Education using the open-source njschooldata R package. There is no hand-entered data, no estimation, and no fabrication. If a number isn’t published by the state, the section says so rather than guessing.

title: About the Data
text: Every figure on this site is fetched directly from the New Jersey Department of Education using the open-source njschooldata R package. There is no hand-entered data, no estimation, and no fabrication. If a number isn’t published by the state, the section says so rather than guessing.

title: About the Data
text:
Data sources



Section
Source
Years shown




Enrollment & demographics
NJ DOE Fall Enrollment files
2015–2026


Spending
Taxpayers’ Guide to Educational Spending (TGES)
2014–2024 (actuals)


Graduation
NJ DOE Four-Year Adjusted Cohort Graduation Rate
2013–2025


High-school proficiency
NJGPA (NJ Graduation Proficiency Assessment)
2022–2025


Chronic absenteeism
NJ School Performance Reports
2019–2024


Staffing ratios
NJ School Performance Reports
latest year

title: About the Data
text:
How to read the comparisons
Most charts compare a district to its peer group median (the dashed line). Peers are defined by District Factor Group (DFG) — the NJ DOE’s socioeconomic classification of districts, lettered A (highest need) through J (most affluent). Comparing a district to demographically similar districts is far more meaningful than comparing it to the statewide average. Charter and special districts without a DFG are compared against all districts.
Percentiles (“72nd percentile among 103 DFG I peers”) describe where a district falls within its peer group on that metric, from the latest available year.

title: About the Data
text:
Caveats and suppression

Small cells are suppressed at the source. The NJ DOE withholds counts for very small groups to protect student privacy; these appear as missing, never as zero or an estimate.
Spending is district-level. TGES reports traditional districts, not individual charters, so charter profiles omit the spending section.
Per-pupil spending uses “actuals.” Figures lag ~2 years behind the budget year, consistent with how TGES reports settled costs.
Some districts don’t enroll every grade. A K–8 district has no graduation rate; a profile marks such sections as not applicable rather than showing an empty chart.

title: About the Data
text:
Reproduce any number
Everything here is one function call away:
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("almartin82/njschooldata")
library(njschooldata)

fetch_enr(2026, tidy = TRUE)        # enrollment, all districts
fetch_grad_rate(2025)               # graduation rates
fetch_tges(2024)                    # spending (Taxpayers' Guide)
fetch_absence(2024, level = "district")  # chronic absenteeism
The full data engine — 80+ fetchers covering assessments, finance, staffing, discipline, and more — is documented at almartin82.github.io/njschooldata.

title: About the Data
text:
Who made this
Built by Andy Martin on top of njschooldata, the open interface to New Jersey school data. Found an error? Open an issue on GitHub.

All figures on this page are fetched from the New Jersey Department of Education via the open-source njschooldata R package. Nothing is hand-entered or estimated.
